name: Shinto Torii Gate Japan
desc: The iconic entrance that marks the transition from the mundane to the sacred. A universal symbol for Shinto shrines throughout Japan.

name: Traditional Chochin Lantern Japan
desc: Paper lanterns used to light the paths of shrines and temples during evening festivals and rituals in Japan.

name: Praying Hands Worship Japan
desc: Represents the act of offering a prayer (Mairi) at a sanctuary. A gesture of respect and gratitude common in Japan.

name: Shrine Suzu Bell Japan
desc: Large bells rung by worshippers to wake the spirits (Kami) and announce their presence at a shrine in Japan.

name: Purification Temizu Ritual Japan
desc: Represents the 'Temizuya' pavilion where visitors wash their hands and mouths before entering a sacred space in Japan.

name: Sacred White Flower Japan
desc: Often associated with purity and the cherry blossom motifs found in shrine architecture and amulets across Japan.

name: Inari Kitsune Messenger Japan
desc: The fox messenger of Inari, the deity of rice and prosperity. Frequently seen as stone statues at Fushimi Inari in Japan.

name: Kadomatsu New Year Decoration Japan
desc: Traditional bamboo and pine arrangements placed at the gates of shrines and homes to welcome ancestral spirits to Japan.

name: Sacred Sunrise Hatsuhinode Japan
desc: Represents the first sunrise of the year, a moment of deep spiritual significance for those visiting shrines in Japan.

name: Omikuji Fortune Scroll Japan
desc: Random fortunes written on strips of paper. Worshippers tie them to trees at the shrine if the luck is bad in Japan.

name: Omamori Protection Charm Japan
desc: Amulets sold at shrines to provide luck or protection for health, traffic safety, and studies for people in Japan.

name: Sacred Nara Deer Japan
desc: Considered messengers of the gods in Shinto, especially famous at Kasuga Taisha and Nara Park in Japan.
